tidyfault 0.0.0.9
  • Reference

    populate() Function

    populate.Rd

    Converts binary outcomes (0/1) to probability outcomes by replacing 1s with event failure probabilities. The output has the same structure as the input binary outcomes, but with probabilities instead of binary values.

    populate(binary_outcomes, event_probs)

    Arguments

    binary_outcomes

    (Required) Data frame with binary (0/1) event states. Must have one column per basic event (with event names as column names) and optionally a scenario column. Each row represents one scenario.

    event_probs

    (Required) Data frame with two columns: event (event names) and probability (failure probabilities in [0, 1]). Must contain probabilities for all events present in binary_outcomes.

    Value

    A tibble with the same structure as binary_outcomes, where:

    • Values of 1 are replaced with P(event) from event_probs

    • Values of 0 remain as 0

    The scenario column (if present) is preserved unchanged.

    Details

    This function transforms binary event states by replacing occurrences (1s) with their corresponding failure probabilities. For each event column, if the binary value is 1 (event occurred), it is replaced with the failure probability P(event) from event_probs. If the binary value is 0 (event did not occur), it remains 0. This creates a data frame suitable for probabilistic evaluation where only occurring events have probability values.

    See also

    quantify for evaluating fault trees with binary or probabilistic inputs, quantify_prob for computing top event failure probability

    Examples

    library(tidyverse)
library(tidyfault)

data("db_outcomes_binary")
data("db_probs")

# Convert binary outcomes to probability outcomes
populate(db_outcomes_binary, db_probs)
#> # A tibble: 10 × 9
#>    scenario    DC    AF    SF    BF    NF   AUF    HF    MF
#>       <int> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
#>  1        1  0      0.1  0     0.15  0     0     0      0.2
#>  2        2  0      0    0.08  0     0.12  0     0      0.2
#>  3        3  0.05   0    0     0.15  0     0     0      0  
#>  4        4  0      0.1  0     0     0.12  0     0.04   0  
#>  5        5  0      0    0     0.15  0     0.06  0      0.2
#>  6        6  0.05   0    0     0     0.12  0     0      0.2
#>  7        7  0      0.1  0.08  0     0     0     0      0  
#>  8        8  0      0    0     0.15  0     0     0      0.2
#>  9        9  0      0.1  0     0     0.12  0     0      0  
#> 10       10  0.05   0    0     0.15  0     0.06  0      0.2

# Compare: binary has 0/1, probability version has P(event) where binary was 1, 0 otherwise
db_outcomes_binary
#> # A tibble: 10 × 9
#>    scenario    DC    AF    SF    BF    NF   AUF    HF    MF
#>       <int> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
#>  1        1     0     1     0     1     0     0     0     1
#>  2        2     0     0     1     0     1     0     0     1
#>  3        3     1     0     0     1     0     0     0     0
#>  4        4     0     1     0     0     1     0     1     0
#>  5        5     0     0     0     1     0     1     0     1
#>  6        6     1     0     0     0     1     0     0     1
#>  7        7     0     1     1     0     0     0     0     0
#>  8        8     0     0     0     1     0     0     0     1
#>  9        9     0     1     0     0     1     0     0     0
#> 10       10     1     0     0     1     0     1     0     1
populate(db_outcomes_binary, db_probs)
#> # A tibble: 10 × 9
#>    scenario    DC    AF    SF    BF    NF   AUF    HF    MF
#>       <int> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
#>  1        1  0      0.1  0     0.15  0     0     0      0.2
#>  2        2  0      0    0.08  0     0.12  0     0      0.2
#>  3        3  0.05   0    0     0.15  0     0     0      0  
#>  4        4  0      0.1  0     0     0.12  0     0.04   0  
#>  5        5  0      0    0     0.15  0     0.06  0      0.2
#>  6        6  0.05   0    0     0     0.12  0     0      0.2
#>  7        7  0      0.1  0.08  0     0     0     0      0  
#>  8        8  0      0    0     0.15  0     0     0      0.2
#>  9        9  0      0.1  0     0     0.12  0     0      0  
#> 10       10  0.05   0    0     0.15  0     0.06  0      0.2
